title: vicia ludoviciana var. leavenworthii
intro: Vicia ludoviciana var. leavenworthii, also known as Leavenworth's vetch or western vetch, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Vicia ludoviciana var. leavenworthii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
